items.title: NGINX CVE-2026-42945 (CVSS 9.2) Actively Exploited in the Wild
items.summary: A heap buffer overflow in ngx_http_rewrite_module affects NGINX Plus and Open versions 0.6.27 through 1.30.0. Unauthenticated attackers can crash worker processes or achieve remote code execution via crafted HTTP requests. Active exploitation began within days of public disclosure.
items.why: NGINX powers roughly one-third of all web servers globally. A CVSS 9.2 unauthenticated RCE with active in-the-wild exploitation means mass exposure — patch or mitigate immediately.

items.title: CVE-2026-31431 'Copy Fail' — Linux LPE to Root Hits Millions of Cloud Workloads
items.summary: Microsoft Defender identified a high-severity local privilege escalation flaw in the Linux kernel affecting Red Hat, SUSE, Ubuntu, and AWS Linux. The bug allows any low-privilege user to escalate to root, impacting a significant share of Kubernetes clusters and cloud VMs. CISA has added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
items.why: Cloud-native environments running any of the major Linux distributions are at risk. LPE-to-root in containerized or multi-tenant environments can lead to full cluster compromise.

items.title: APT28 (Pawn Storm) Deploys PRISMEX Malware Suite Against NATO Defense Supply Chain
items.summary: Trend Micro reports that Russian-linked Pawn Storm (APT28/Fancy Bear) is targeting the defense supply chain of Ukraine and its NATO allies — Czech Republic, Poland, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, and Turkey — using a new multi-stage malware suite dubbed PRISMEX. The campaign also leveraged a confirmed Windows zero-day (CVE-2026-21513).
items.why: Nation-state offensive campaign with zero-day use against critical NATO-aligned defense infrastructure. PRISMEX's modular design suggests ongoing evolution and long-term persistence objectives.

items.title: APT41 Weaponizes Google Calendar as Covert C2 Channel via ToughProgress Malware
items.summary: Resecurity analysts have documented APT41's ToughProgress malware communicating with operators by creating and modifying Google Calendar events as a command-and-control mechanism. This Living-off-Trusted-Sites (LoTS) technique abuses legitimate Google infrastructure to blend C2 traffic with normal enterprise web traffic, bypassing traditional network detection.
items.why: Abusing trusted SaaS platforms for C2 is a growing blind spot — most security tools whitelist Google services. This technique undermines perimeter-based detection and is likely to be adopted by other APT groups.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Attackers Hand Off Access in 22 Seconds, 714 New Malware Families Tracked
items.summary: Based on 500,000+ hours of frontline incident response in 2025, Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report reveals attackers are drastically accelerating internal pivoting — handing off compromised access between tools and operators in as little as 22 seconds. GTIG tracked 714 new malware families, up from 632 in 2024, including PROMPTFLUX and PROMPTSTEAL which query LLMs mid-execution to evade behavioral detection.
items.why: The 22-second handoff stat underscores that dwell-time-based detection is increasingly obsolete. LLM-querying malware is a novel evasion class that traditional sandboxes and signature engines are not designed to catch.
